reviews.review_description: Really cool! Loved the cave and the enviroments. The waves and the final cave were amazing (dat butt!). I got stuck next to the tower in the snow map, had to press back button. Also, infinite walk on water at a slow pace was not fun. Wish there were crazy stories and more common dreams like falling or flying.

developer_response.body: Thanks for the review and the feedback. I will fix the bug you encountered. I will also look into a flying dream.

reviews.review_description: But I need to be able to rotate my view.  Is that possible with a game pad?

developer_response.body: It is possible. All you have to do is more your secondary joystick.

reviews.review_description: The worlds were cool, sometimes I would go wow or feel relaxed. Looks very refined(I like how u can lay in that bed,works good if u do it in real life as well)

developer_response.body: Thanks for the review. I'm glad you liked it!

reviews.review_description: Sorry but pretty boring after 5 minutes.  Very little interaction,  and I lost interest very quickly.

developer_response.body: Thanks for the review. I can see why you wouldn't like it, and I'm sure many others have the same views as you do. Different things appeal to different people.

reviews.review_title: A game with first person controls!
reviews.review_description: This is a fun little sampling of virtual reality with a Bluetooth controller in first person. Really enjoyable!
This game is beautiful. Easy to maneuver through. Good for folks like me who is not an aggressive gamer just a casual player who wants a relaxing experience.

developer_response.body: Thanks for the review! I'm glad you like it.

reviews.review_description: It won't open, I have an S7 edge and it "closes unexpectedly" whenever I try and launch the app.

developer_response.body: I have just finished testing the app on a S7 and a S7 edge with no problems, so it is something on your end. Make sure you have the newest version of the Oculus app and that everything is up to date.

reviews.review_description: The game hace a great graphics and scenarios, it's super relax this game, stressless app, you should make more dreams in this game.

This isn't the most technical of Go experiences, nor is it particularly challenging. What it offers is simple exploration of some beautifully designed worlds, some frightening, some comforting, some awe-inspiring. We are mere steps away from exploring Jurassic Park or seeing Avatar's Pandora in the flesh. 

On the downside, the game lacks tactile interaction. You really want to manipulate things like space ships or open doors to a mushroom house. Haptic feedback would be fun. Regardless, it's great fun.

Really good game 
My girlfriend loves it

developer_response.body: Thank you for the review! I'm glad you two like it.

reviews.review_description: Should have a crystal count to know how many you have collected   and what's the purpose of the weapon in the Chinese place?

developer_response.body: Thanks for the review. There will be a crystal count wherever you need crystals to proceed. Also there is not really any purpose to anything in the dreams.

reviews.review_description: The new update works now, would like more to explore and more to do in each realm. Decent app �

developer_response.body: I'm glad its working for you now. Thanks for the feedback.

reviews.review_description:
This makes me think of the OASIS from Ready Player One. On that I think it would be incredible for more.people to add worlds to this app and help it grow into a universe of worlds :D 

I would love to see NPCs in the game world like the giant in the castle world and more collectables other than the gems. 

Overall I love the app and its premise. Just want more interactivity and some more worlds to explore!

developer_response.body: Thanks for the review. User created worlds would be amazing, but currently not possible for me to implement. There would be more NPCs and lively worlds, but it's very hard to achieve 60fps with big detailed worlds and characters to interact with. I do plan to add more worlds in the future though, and I'll try to have more NPCs as well.

reviews.review_description: Scenes like a dream, control like a nightmare. Needs update badly.

Just the type of game I was looking for. The swipe movement doesn't seem to work good on oculus go. Tap movement works ok. Also could you have option of swipe or click turning left and right instead of having to look around as I like to play in bed and can't turn around. Thanks

developer_response.body: Thanks for the review! I don't have a Go for testing so I don't know how the controls work for it, but good to know that taping works well. I went ahead and implemented what you asked for. You can now rotate by pressing on the sides of the controller. Enjoy!

reviews.review_description:
If you're like me and simply like to explore & wonder you'll like this self paced multiple dream world. Some are peaceful. Others are kind of Erie. 

Find and collect gems to unlock other dream islands. 

Honestly, this app could be expanded to let independent cgi artist create & add more islands. Some people just want to explore.

P.s. It would be nice to be able to play or see something on the tv or stereo in the bedroom...

developer_response.body: Thanks for the review! In a future update, there could be a video playing on the TV instead of static. The speaker does play music (Moonlight Sonata) if you interact with it.

reviews.review_description:
There's a weird visual glitch in the menu area. The edges of a few of the portals flicker black and white when they're at the edge of my visual field. Selecting stuff by clicking the touchpad instead of the trigger button feels really unintuitive, and not making use of two different buttons (touchpad & trigger) seems like a huge missed opportunity (and a very weird decision to be honest). I didn't find a way to speed up at will. I'm guessing there could be a way to do that since you have an unused button. Would be nice because I often run into tiny ledges I can't seem to walk over and then I have to wait until movement speeds up again. What about maybe a swipe down to move backwards? I was often too close to the portal to click it so could come in handy. The fish disappeared and reappeared next to the statue's left ear. In the dream with the mushrooms all the triangular planes on the mushrooms kept flickering. In the dream with the floating rocks and the purple sky the plants only rendered when I came really close. And the one dream shard next to a vehicle was really hard to pick up because I couldn't move close enough.

Tested on SM-N950F.

Edit:
Not using the trigger button is mostly super weird in the settings menu. It's what people are used to using nowadays for that use so maybe you should set it to have the same function as touchpad button there.
For moving it's different since neither one is really intuitive for that so either one of them is fine.
Yeah, I already guessed your response about the fish issue. Would be really difficult to perfectly line it up at the edge I guess... The fish seemed super predictable but I guess that's also to make it easier on the older phones.
What about maybe a mode where you walk in the direction your controller is pointed at instead of the direction you're looking at? I suppose you would more easily get people motion sick but that's already the case with free movement. Teleportation free movement seemed much more difficult to use than in most games where they use it, but it did work.

developer_response.body: Thanks for the additional input. I could set the trigger to use the same functionality as clicking. In the next update I can have an option to use the controller direction as well. I will admit other games do teleportation better. I implemented it a while ago before good practices were around.

reviews.review_title: best vr exploration game to date
reviews.review_description: Can't wait for more

developer_response.body: Thank you for the review! I'm very happy that you think that.

reviews.review_description: A little too short, didn't really have an ending. Some objects in the game didn't have much of a purpose; the interactions felt odd. Altogether a great idea. The lighthouse level with the roiling sea really got me. I also enjoyed Stonehenge.

developer_response.body: Thanks for the feedback. It is pretty short. There will be more content in the future though. I'm also glad you enjoyed the worlds.

reviews.review_title: I thought I'd be requesting a refund
reviews.review_description: I love this app need  have I fond an app with such a great meditation factor _works best with game pad--- but either way it's a great way to dream while awake
